headline: Accenture forecasts quarterly revenue below estimates on cautious enterprise spending
summary: Accenture forecast quarterly revenue below estimates on Thursday, as clients remain cautious on ‌spending on large IT transformation projects amid an ‌uncertain economic environment. Shares of the Dublin, Ireland-based company were down more ​than 3% in premarket trading. The company has been navigating a challenging economic environment, as clients delay large digital transformation projects and prioritize cost control and short-term initiatives.

content.title: Accenture forecasts quarterly revenue below estimates on cautious enterprise spending

The company has been navigating a challenging economic environment, as clients delay large digital transformation projects and prioritize cost control and short-term initiatives.

headline: Accenture (NYSE:ACN) Posts Better-Than-Expected Sales In Q1 CY2026
summary: Global professional services company Accenture (NYSE:ACN) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 8.3% year on year to $18.04 billion. On the other hand, next quarter’s revenue guidance of $18.68 billion was less impressive, coming in 0.5% below analysts’ estimates. Its GAAP profit of $2.93 per share was 3.4% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

On the other hand, next quarter’s revenue guidance of $18.68 billion was less impressive, coming in 0.5% below analysts’ estimates. Its GAAP profit of $2.93 per share was 3.4% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Accenture beats quarterly revenue estimates on strong AI demand
summary: Accenture beat quarterly revenue estimates on Thursday on strong demand for services that help businesses adopt artificial intelligence ‌and move to the cloud, lifting the IT consulting firm's shares more ‌than 3%.  Global consulting firms such as Accenture and Cognizant are seeing robust demand from companies seeking ​external technology partners to automate complex tasks.  Acquisitions of fast-growing firms and AI-focused assets will account for roughly $5 billion in spending this year, Accenture CEO Julie Sweet said, as the company leverages its strong finances ‌to scale up.

content.title: Accenture beats quarterly revenue estimates on strong AI demand
content.summary: Accenture beat quarterly revenue estimates on Thursday on strong demand for services that help businesses adopt artificial intelligence ‌and move to the cloud, lifting the IT consulting firm's shares more ‌than 3%.  Global consulting firms such as Accenture and Cognizant are seeing robust demand from companies seeking ​external technology partners to automate complex tasks.  Acquisitions of fast-growing firms and AI-focused assets will account for roughly $5 billion in spending this year, Accenture CEO Julie Sweet said, as the company leverages its strong finances ‌to scale up.

headline: Alibaba stock falls despite AI outlook, Accenture lowers guidance
summary: Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre and Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman take a closer look at some of Thursday morning's trending tickers and stories. Alibaba (BABA) stock is plunging after the company reported revenue that fell short of expectations, though analysts are still bullish on Alibaba's artificial intelligence (AI) outlook. Accenture (ACN) lowered its full-year guidance. Elliott Investment Management is building a stake in Invisalign-maker Align Technology (ALGN).

content.title: Alibaba stock falls despite AI outlook, Accenture lowers guidance
content.description:
Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre and Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman take a closer look at some of Thursday morning's trending tickers and stories.

Alibaba (BABA) stock is plunging after the company reported revenue that fell short of expectations, though analysts are still bullish on Alibaba's artificial intelligence (AI) outlook.

Accenture (ACN) lowered its full-year guidance.

Elliott Investment Management is building a stake in Invisalign-maker Align Technology (ALGN).
content.summary: Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre and Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman take a closer look at some of Thursday morning's trending tickers and stories. Alibaba (BABA) stock is plunging after the company reported revenue that fell short of expectations, though analysts are still bullish on Alibaba's artificial intelligence (AI) outlook. Accenture (ACN) lowered its full-year guidance. Elliott Investment Management is building a stake in Invisalign-maker Align Technology (ALGN).
